must_not_assert: that an account was compromised or a breach occurred (all 50 events A0..EV-049 return sub_status 0xc0000064 = target user name does not exist, and no 4624 success event is present in the package)
that a valid credential was obtained or that lateral movement followed (no success event and no post-authentication activity are present in the package)
that the activity is confirmed malicious or attacker-driven, or that the sprayed user list was 'entirely' invalid beyond the 50 target_user_name values actually shown

the_trap: A burst of 50 failed-logon events (A0 + EV-001..EV-049, all event_code 4625) inside ~181 ms on host-01.corp.local is a genuine credential-spray indicator: one subject_user_name (user-01) and one process (C:\\Tools\\[x]\\[x].exe) generate attempts against 50 distinct random-looking target_user_name values (e.g. VFRKKMDG, AACECLCL). But every event returns sub_status 0xc0000064 (target user name does not exist) with status 0xc000006d and failure_reason %%2313, and no 4624 success event is present anywhere in the package (deterministic_derivations is empty). All attempts observed here missed non-existent usernames, so no valid-account hit is evidenced and no compromise can be confirmed from this bundle. logon_type is 2 with ip_address '-' (local/console-style origin rather than a remote source), a provenance detail that does not by itself resolve intent. The model must not over-escalate on the spray pattern alone.
rationale: The 50-event burst (A0 + EV-001..EV-049) shares one subject_user_name (user-01), one process, and one workstation_name (host-01) firing at 50 distinct target_user_name values in ~181 ms, which is a real spray-style indicator. The mitigating facts are that every event carries sub_status 0xc0000064 (user does not exist) with failure_reason %%2313, and no 4624 success event appears in the package (deterministic_derivations is empty). A real indicator, but with all sampled attempts failing against non-existent usernames and no success event present, a compromise or valid-credential hit cannot be confirmed: suspicious, low-to-medium impact, investigate without over-escalating.
